# Codebase-to-Figma UI Translation

This reference guide provides instructions and scripts for parsing code structures (stylesheets, configurations, layouts) and recreating them programmatically inside Figma, as well as setting up Figma Code Connect mappings.

---

## 1. Extracting Theme Configurations from Code

To start the translation, extract style definitions from the repository files.

### 1.1. Parsing CSS Variables
Write a parser to crawl target stylesheets (e.g., `theme.css`, `globals.css`) to extract variable rules.
```javascript
// Example extraction map generated by parser
const parsedStyles = {
  colors: {
    "bg-default": "#F8FAFC",
    "text-primary": "#0F172A",
    "brand-primary": "#3B82F6"
  },
  typography: {
    "font-sans": "'Inter', sans-serif",
    "text-base": "16px",
    "line-height-base": "24px"
  },
  spacing: {
    "space-1": "4px",
    "space-2": "8px",
    "space-4": "16px"
  }
};
```

---

## 2. Recreating UI Elements programmatically

The agent builds elements using the Figma Plugin API via `use_figma`.

### 2.1. Initializing and Binding Text Nodes
To render typographic scales, the agent must load the font families prior to creating text layers:

```javascript
// Load the target font, then write text nodes inside use_figma
async function createTextNode(textValue, x, y, sizeVar, familyVar) {
  // Load standard system font fallback
  await figma.loadFontAsync({ family: "Inter", style: "Regular" });
  
  const textNode = figma.createText();
  textNode.x = x;
  textNode.y = y;
  textNode.characters = textValue;
  textNode.fontSize = 16; // base default
  
  // Bind the font size to the Figma Typography Variable
  const sizeVariable = (await figma.variables.getLocalVariablesAsync()).find(v => v.name === sizeVar);
  if (sizeVariable) {
    textNode.setBoundVariable("fontSize", sizeVariable.id);
  }
  
  return textNode;
}
```

### 2.2. Building Layout Hierarchies from DOM Tree
Rebuild UI elements from code files (HTML template files, JSX nodes) by traversing nested hierarchies:

```javascript
// Example structural tree parsed from a React/HTML structure
const htmlTree = {
  type: "DIV",
  props: { className: "flex flex-col gap-4 p-6 bg-default rounded-lg" },
  children: [
    { type: "SPAN", props: { className: "text-lg font-sans text-primary" }, text: "Card Title" },
    { type: "BUTTON", props: { className: "px-4 py-2 bg-brand text-white rounded" }, text: "Action" }
  ]
};

// Rebuild inside Figma
async function drawHtmlNode(node, parentFrame) {
  let element;
  if (node.type === "SPAN" || node.type === "P" || node.type === "H1") {
    element = await createTextNode(node.text || "", 0, 0);
  } else {
    element = figma.createFrame();
    element.name = node.type;
    applyCSSClasses(element, node.props.className);
  }
  
  if (parentFrame) {
    parentFrame.appendChild(element);
  }
  
  if (node.children) {
    for (const child of node.children) {
      await drawHtmlNode(child, element);
    }
  }
  return element;
}
```

---

## 3. Auto Layout Alignments Mapping

Translate Tailwind/CSS spacing classes directly to Figma frame properties:
- `p-4` (16px padding) $\rightarrow$ Set `paddingLeft, paddingRight, paddingTop, paddingBottom = 16`.
- `flex-col` $\rightarrow$ Set `layoutMode = "VERTICAL"`.
- `gap-3` (12px gap) $\rightarrow$ Set `itemSpacing = 12`.
- `items-center` $\rightarrow$ Set `counterAxisAlignItems = "CENTER"`.
- `justify-between` $\rightarrow$ Set `primaryAxisAlignItems = "SPACE_BETWEEN"`.

---

## 4. Figma Code Connect Integration

Figma Code Connect links UI components in production to their Figma designs, showing developers correct code snippets directly in Dev Mode.

### 4.1. Configuration (`figma.config.json`)
Sit this configuration file in your project root to tell the Figma CLI where to scan:
```json
{
  "codeConnect": {
    "include": ["src/components/**/*.figma.tsx"],
    "label": "React",
    "language": "typescript"
  }
}
```

### 4.2. Creating the Component Mapping (`Button.figma.tsx`)
Generate mapping files next to components to bind code properties to Figma variants:

```tsx
import figma from '@figma/code-connect/react';
import { Button } from './Button'; // Import React component

figma.connect(
  Button,
  'https://www.figma.com/design/YOUR_FILE_KEY/Your-File-Name?node-id=123:456',
  {
    // Define the custom code snippet shown in Figma Dev Mode
    example: (props) => (
      <Button 
        variant={props.variant}
        size={props.size}
        disabled={props.disabled}
      >
        {props.label}
      </Button>
    ),
    // Bind Figma component properties to React component properties
    props: {
      label: figma.string('Label'),
      disabled: figma.boolean('Disabled'),
      size: figma.enum('Size', {
        'Small': 'sm',
        'Medium': 'md',
        'Large': 'lg'
      }),
      variant: figma.enum('Variant', {
        'Primary': 'primary',
        'Secondary': 'secondary',
        'Outline': 'outline'
      })
    }
  }
);
```
Ensure files are checked in and updated during token synchronization runs to ensure Dev Mode remains updated with code changes.
